recipes.title: Quick Onion Pasta Sauce (Demo)
recipes.description: A sample recipe used to demo the player: step-by-step chapters, loop mode, and voice control. Swap in your own video and steps whenever you're ready.
recipes.intro: A weeknight staple Joe's made hundreds of times — sweet caramelized onions and garlic simmered down into a sauce that tastes like it took all day, in under twenty minutes.
tips: Low and slow on the onions — don't rush the caramelization or you'll lose the sweetness.
Add a splash of pasta water at the end to loosen the sauce and help it cling to the noodles.
This sauce freezes well — double the batch and save half for a night you don't feel like cooking.

steps.label: Mince the onions
steps.direction: Thinly slice both onions into even half-moons so they cook down at the same rate.

steps.label: Heat the pan
steps.direction: Warm the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it shimmers.

steps.label: Sauté the onions
steps.direction: Add the onions and a pinch of salt, stirring occasionally, until soft and golden — about 10 minutes.

steps.label: Add the garlic
steps.direction: Stir in the garlic and red pepper flakes and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

steps.label: Simmer the sauce
steps.direction: Pour in the crushed tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until thickened.
